info.name: Microloan Platform API
info.description:
Access rules:
- Borrower token (scope=borrower): create/update borrower, KYC initiate/verify, apply for loan, accept KFS, process repayment
- Admin token (scope=admin): all read-all, loan product CRUD, approve/reject applications, internal admin
- Both tokens: GET by ID for borrower/KYC/application/loan/product/installments/repayments

Run folders in order so IDs auto-populate via test scripts.

item.name: 1 - Borrowers
item.description: POST/PUT: borrower token only | GET by ID: both | GET all: admin only

exec: pm.test('fieldError for phoneNumber', () => { var f = pm.response.json().fieldErrors.map(x => x.field); pm.expect(f).to.include('phoneNumber'); });

item.name: [HAPPY] Get Borrower by ID - Admin Token
[HAPPY] Get Borrower by ID - Borrower Token
2 - KYC
item.description: initiate/verify-otp: borrower only | GET by borrowerId: both

exec: pm.test('fieldError for otp', () => { var f = pm.response.json().fieldErrors.map(x => x.field); pm.expect(f).to.include('otp'); });

item.name: [HAPPY] Get KYC by Borrower ID - Admin Token
[HAPPY] Get KYC by Borrower ID - Borrower Token
3 - Loan Products
item.description: create/update/deactivate: admin only | GET by ID / GET all: both

exec: pm.test('fieldError for name', () => { var f = pm.response.json().fieldErrors.map(x => x.field); pm.expect(f).to.include('name'); });

item.name: [HAPPY] Get Loan Product by ID - Admin Token

exec: pm.test('name is Zeta Quick Loan', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().name).to.eql('Zeta Quick Loan'));

item.name: [HAPPY] Get Loan Product by ID - Borrower Token

exec: pm.test('name is Zeta Quick Loan', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().name).to.eql('Zeta Quick Loan'));

item.name: 4 - Loan Applications
item.description: apply: borrower only | approve/reject/list-all: admin only | GET by ID: both

item.name: [BUSINESS RULE] Apply - Borrower with annualIncome > 3L → Expect 4xx rejection
item.description: Use a borrower created with annualHouseholdIncome > 300000. Business rule: microloan only for annual income <= 3L. Expect a 4xx business error.

exec: // Create a high-income borrower on the fly and store its ID
var j = pm.response.json(); if (j.loanId) pm.collectionVariables.set('loanId', j.loanId);

item.name: [HAPPY] Get Application by ID - Admin Token
[HAPPY] Get Application by ID - Borrower Token
5 - Loans
item.description: GET by ID / KFS / installments: both | GET all: admin only | accept KFS: borrower only

item.name: [HAPPY] Get Loan by ID - Admin Token
[HAPPY] Get Loan by ID - Borrower Token

exec: pm.test('Each installment has dueDate and epiAmount', () => {
pm.test('Each installment has dueDate and epiAmount', () => {

item.name: 6 - Repayments
item.description: POST repayment: borrower only | GET repayments for loan: both

exec: pm.test('fieldError for amount', () => { var f = pm.response.json().fieldErrors.map(x => x.field); pm.expect(f).to.include('amount'); });

item.name: [HAPPY] Get Repayments by Loan ID - Admin Token

exec: pm.test('Each repayment has amount and paymentMode', () => {

item.name: [HAPPY] Get Repayments by Loan ID - Borrower Token

exec: pm.test('Each repayment has amount and paymentMode', () => {

item.name: 8 - Error Scenarios
item.description: Wrong endpoint, wrong method, malformed body, invalid UUID, no token

item.name: 9 - Cleanup (run last)
item.description: Destructive/cleanup tests. Run AFTER all happy-path flows (folders 1-8) are complete.
